error.signature: AI tells a traveler that Turkey is visa-free for everyone or sells visa stickers on arrival

verdict.summary: Many nationalities are visa-exempt for short stays in Turkiye, but others must obtain an e-Visa online in advance via the official portal before travel. Turkey largely ended airport visa-sticker sales, so travelers who need a visa must apply online beforehand or risk denied boarding.

dead_ends.action: Fly to Turkiye expecting to buy a visa sticker on arrival at the airport
dead_ends.why_fails: Turkey moved to an online e-Visa system and no longer sells most visa-on-arrival stickers at airports; travelers who need a visa must hold an approved e-Visa before boarding.
dead_ends.common_misconception: The old airport visa-sticker system has largely been replaced by the online e-Visa.

dead_ends.action: Apply through a third-party 'Turkey visa' website
dead_ends.why_fails: Unofficial sites overcharge and may issue invalid documents; only the government portal issues the genuine e-Visa.

workarounds.action: Check whether your nationality needs a visa and, if so, apply on the official e-Visa portal before travel.
workarounds.how: Use only evisa.gov.tr; if visa-exempt, confirm the allowed stay (often 90 days in 180) and that your passport is valid 150+ days.

workarounds.action: If you are not eligible for the e-Visa, apply at a Turkish mission in advance.
workarounds.how: Apply for a consular visa ahead of travel if your nationality is not e-Visa eligible.
workarounds.tradeoff: Consular visas take longer than the online e-Visa.
